yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday delivered a solid brick session: 24.9mi bike at HR 124 (TSS 42) followed by 4.1mi run at HR 144 (TSS 40). The combined 82 TSS is quality work, and the run heart rate suggests good effort control off the bike. This was brick workout #11 in the build, maintaining critical neuromuscular patterning for Oregon.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's planned 45-min bike + 15-min brick run, but monitor energy carefully given last night's poor sleep (score 64, only 6.3h, low REM at 24min). Keep the bike easy-moderate and the run truly easy — this is about movement quality before Sunday's race, not fitness gains.
weekProgress: Week 10 of 10 sits at 82 TSS of 300 target (27%) with 4 days remaining including today. This is expected and on-track given the back-loaded schedule — Sunday's SDIT race will be the week's anchor session. All scheduled sessions completed so far.
keyInsight: Last night's compromised sleep (64 score, 6.3h, 24min REM, 91% SpO2) creates a recovery deficit heading into Sunday's International distance race — today's session must be conservative to preserve race-day sharpness.
